info.description: Headless Fleet API. Agents, integrations, auth providers/sessions/tokens, skills, triggers, trigger templates, and threads.
info.title: Fleet API

get.description: List the agents you have access to. Use `next_cursor` from the response to page through results.

post.description: Create an agent, optionally seeded with an initial file tree. The write is atomic — validation runs before any persistence. The full file tree is omitted from the response by default; pass `?include=files` to include it.

requestBody.description: Agent metadata, config, and optional file tree

post.description: Count agents matching the provided filters.
Search agents by metadata, graph ID, or name. Use `next_cursor` from the response to page through results.

delete.description: Delete an agent and its file tree.

patch.description: Update an agent. All fields are optional; omitted fields are unchanged. Provided top-level fields replace the existing value in full. Entries under `files` are created or replaced by path; paths listed in `deleted_paths` are removed. The call is atomic — on validation failure no changes are applied. The file tree is omitted from the response by default; pass `?include=files` to include it.

post.description: Create a thread to hold a conversation. Subsequent runs are started on it via `POST /v1/fleet/threads/{thread_id}/runs`.
Count threads matching the provided filters.
Search threads by metadata, status, or state values. Use `next_cursor` from the response to page through results.

delete.description: Delete a thread and its state.

patch.description: Update a thread. All fields are optional; omitted fields are unchanged.

post.description: Resume a thread paused for human interrupt without sending new input. Execution continues from where it stopped.
Start a run on a thread. The request body must include `agent_id` to identify the agent to execute.
Start a run on a thread and stream output as Server-Sent Events. The connection stays open until the run completes; the client is responsible for closing it. The request body must include `agent_id`.

200.description:
SSE stream of run events. Each event is `id: <n>
event: <name>
data: <json>

`.

get.description: Get the latest state (most recent checkpoint) for a thread.

parameters.description: Include subgraph states in the response
parameters.name: subgraphs

post.description: Add or update state on a thread. Optionally targets a specific checkpoint, and can attribute the update to a named node via `as_node`.

get.description: Join the live event stream for a thread. The stream emits events from each run executed on the thread sequentially and remains open until the client disconnects. Pass `Last-Event-ID` to resume from a known event.

parameters.description: ID of the last event received. Pass to resume from that point; pass `-` to resume from the beginning.
parameters.name: Last-Event-ID

parameters.description: Stream modes to subscribe to. Comma-separated. Supported: lifecycle, run_modes, state_update
parameters.name: stream_modes

200.description:
SSE stream of thread events. Each event is `id: <n>
event: <name>
data: <json>

`.

get.description: List the OAuth providers configured for your organization.

post.description: Register an OAuth provider for your organization. Users can then start authorization sessions against it via `POST /v1/fleet/auth-sessions`.
Discover an OAuth provider's metadata from an MCP server URL and register it for your organization in one step.
post.summary: Discover and register an OAuth provider

delete.description: Delete an OAuth provider. Existing tokens issued under it are revoked.

get.description: Get an OAuth provider by ID.

patch.description: Update an OAuth provider. Sent fields replace stored values; absent fields are unchanged.

post.description: Start an OAuth flow. If the user is already authorized, returns a completed session referencing the existing token. Otherwise returns a pending session with a verification URL the user must visit to finish authorization.

get.description: Get the status of an in-flight authorization session. Poll until `status` is `completed`, or the session expires.
List your active connection tokens. Optionally filter by `provider_id` or `agent_id`.

post.description: Revoke connection tokens matching a filter. `provider_id` is required to prevent accidental mass revocation.

delete.description: Revoke a connection token by ID. The user is disconnected from the service it authorized.

patch.description: Update a connection token. Sent fields replace stored values; absent fields are unchanged.

get.description: List the integrations available to your workspace. Filter by `source` (for example `langchain` or `custom`).

post.description: Create a custom integration in your workspace.

patch.description: Update a custom integration. The `url` field is immutable; sent fields replace stored values, absent fields are unchanged.

put.description: Replace the integration's full list of supported auth methods. Any method present today but absent from the request is removed.

get.description: List the skills in your workspace. Use `next_cursor` from the response to page through results.

post.description: Create a skill in your workspace with the supplied file tree. The call is atomic — if the file commit fails, no skill is created.

requestBody.description: Skill name and initial file tree

delete.description: Delete a skill and all its files.

get.description: Get a skill by ID, including its full file tree.

put.description: Replace a skill's file tree in full. Any file present today but absent from the request body is deleted. The call is atomic — on validation failure no changes are applied.

get.description: List the trigger templates available to your workspace. Use `next_cursor` from the response to page through results.
Get a trigger template by ID.
List triggers in your workspace. Use the optional `agent_id` and `template_id` filters to narrow results. Use `next_cursor` from the response to page through results.

post.description: Create a trigger and bind it to an agent. The agent association is fixed at create time; to point a trigger at a different agent, delete and recreate.

patch.description: Update a trigger. All fields are optional; omitted fields are unchanged. The `template_id` and `agent_id` are immutable — to change them, delete and recreate.

tags.description: An agent is a configured graph with its own files, configuration, and metadata.
tags.name: agents

tags.description: An OAuth provider defines how a third-party service authorizes connections on behalf of your users.
tags.name: auth-providers

tags.description: An auth session is an in-flight OAuth flow that connects an end user to a provider.
tags.name: auth-sessions

tags.description: A connection token represents a user's authorized access to a third-party service.
tags.name: auth-tokens

tags.description: An integration is a third-party service available to your workspace, with one or more supported auth methods.
tags.name: integrations

tags.description: A skill is a reusable, versioned bundle of files that agents can load at runtime.
tags.name: skills

tags.description: A thread holds the accumulated state of a conversation across one or more runs.
tags.name: threads

tags.description: A trigger template defines a kind of trigger available to your workspace (cron, webhook, Slack bot, etc.).
tags.name: trigger-templates

tags.description: A trigger fires an agent in response to an event — a schedule, an inbound message, or a webhook.
tags.name: triggers
